name: Duero Trail (GR 14)
name_original: Sardón de Duero desde Quintanilla de Onésimo — circular a orillas del Duero
description: Hike a moderate 11.4-mile (18.4 km) circular route from Quintanilla de Onésimo, exploring the Duero River banks, vineyards, and historical l
region_intro: Hiking around Olivares De Duero offers exploration of the scenic landscapes within the Duero River basin. The region is characterized by rolling plains, extensive vineyards, and pine forests, shaped by a continental climate. The Duero River is a central natural feature, providing tranquil riverine environments. These physical features create a diverse backdrop for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Olivares De Duero The most popular hiking route is Atalaya Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This route leads to Pico Atalaya, offering panoramic views of the Ribera del Duero. Another top favourite among local hikers is Duero Trail (GR 14), a moderate 11.4 miles (18.4 km) path. This trail follows the Duero River, providing riverine landscapes. Local hikers also love the Sendero del Valcorba (PRC-VA 21) — circular Santibáñez de Valcorba, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Olivares De Duero is defined by rolling plains, vineyards, and the Duero River. The network of trails offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Olivares De Duero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Olivares De Duero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Olivares de Duero?
A: Hiking in Olivares de Duero offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter picturesque rolling plains, extensive vineyards characteristic of the Ribera del Duero wine region, and tranquil riverine environments along the Duero River. Some trails also lead through pine forests, providing varied scenery.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Olivares de Duero?
A: Yes, Olivares de Duero offers several easy trails. There are 7 easy routes available, making it suitable for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077527970/atalaya-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.3 km long and takes about 1 hour 31 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find trails with panoramic viewpoints in the region?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077527970/atalaya-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads to Pico Atalaya, offering a superb panoramic view of almost the entire Ribera del Duero from Valladolid, including the expansive vineyards and the town of Olivares de Duero.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077535602/sendero-del-valcorba-prc-va-21-circular-santibanez-de-valcorba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Valcorba (PRC®-VA 21)&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8.3 km moderate trail. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077524968/sendero-de-negredo-prc-va-32-circular-desde-traspinedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de Negredo (PRC-VA 32)&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.6 km circular route.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can be seen near the hiking trails?
A: The hiking experience is enriched by historical and cultural elements. You can encounter landmarks such as the 15th-century Gothic Parish Church of San Pelayo and the 17th-century hermitage of Nuestra Señora de Estrella. The magnificent 16th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge of Olivares de Duero&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable attraction along the Duero River.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Olivares de Duero?
A: Olivares de Duero experiences a continental climate. While hot, dry summers and cold winters are typical, the spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for hiking, with milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors, especially in the vineyards.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Duero River?
A: Yes, the Duero River is a central natural feature, and some trails follow its course. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084789912/duero-trail-gr-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Trail (GR 14)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 18.4 km path that specifically follows the river, offering tranquil riverine landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Olivares de Duero?
A: The routes in Olivares de Duero are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views of the vineyards, and the well-maintained paths that offer a peaceful outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any natural springs or water features along the trails?
A: Yes, some trails offer natural springs. For example, along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077527970/atalaya-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya Trail&lt;/a&gt;, you can discover natural springs like the Fuente de Vallelado, nestled within a grove of oaks, providing a refreshing stop.

Q: What other attractions are nearby that I could visit after a hike?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several nearby attractions. These include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3589685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Armedilla Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4102610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafuerte de Esgueva Castle&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4011321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Hermitage of Traspinedo&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the region's rich history.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Olivares de Duero?
A: There are over 18 hiking routes available around Olivares de Duero, catering to various skill levels from easy walks to moderate hikes.
tips: Interesting winery with a boss who is happy to let visitors participate in his quite unusual marketing ideas as part of a wine tasting.
Religious sanctuary from the 16th century in a Gothic style, although only the main chapel and the previous section of the original building remain.
Town of Valladolid located on the banks of the Duero channel. It has a square in the center with the church of San Juan Bautista (in Gothic style but restored with brick).

faqs.question: How difficult is the Sardón de Duero circular hike from Quintanilla de Onésimo?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as a moderate hike. It requires a good level of fitness but is generally considered suitable for all skill levels due to its easily accessible paths. The route is approximately 18.4 kilometers (11.4 miles) long.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain predominantly follows hiking paths, with sections of gravel, unpaved tracks, and some asphalt roads. A significant portion runs along the "caballón," an embankment separating the Duero River from the Canal del Duero. The path is generally firm and pleasant, winding through a rich riparian forest.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: It typically takes around 4 hours and 40 minutes to complete the entire circular route.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll follow the 19th-century Canal del Duero and pass through a prominent wine region with many vineyards, such as Viñedos y Bodegas Cepa Alta. You can also see historical structures like monumental bridges and the 16th-century Church of San Juan Bautista of Sardón de Duero. The Church of San Millán is also a highlight near the trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers splendid riverside landscapes, especially in autumn, when the riparian forest and vineyards display vibrant colors. Spring is also a beautiful time with lush greenery.

faqs.question: Is the Sardón de Duero from Quintanilla de Onésimo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. There are no specific restrictions mentioned, but always ensure your dog is under control, especially when passing through vineyards or near livestock, and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this public circular route along the Duero River banks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Quintanilla de Onésimo?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Quintanilla de Onésimo is the recommended starting point. You should be able to find public parking within the village to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular path is part of the larger Ruta del Duero (GR 14), a long-distance natural trail that follows the Duero River. It also covers sections of Etapa 13: Quintanilla de Onésimo – Tudela de Duero and briefly touches upon Etapa 12: Peñafiel – Quintanilla de Onésimo.

faqs.question: What kind of natural scenery and wildlife can I expect?
faqs.answer: You can expect beautiful riverside landscapes with a rich riparian forest featuring trees like poplars, willows, alders, hazelnuts, and ash trees. This creates a dense, almost jungle-like environment in some sections. The area is also known for its vineyards. While specific wildlife sightings aren't guaranteed, riverbanks often host various bird species and small mammals.

waypoints.name: Church of San Juan Bautista of Sardón de Duero
